summary: Tested Enterprise Self-Healing System feature. All 24 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend Enterprise tab verified with all sections working: System Status HEALTHY, Service Health (Backend/Frontend/MongoDB all healthy), System Metrics (CPU/Memory/Disk), Learning Engine, Rollback Manager, and Critical Module Protection.

issues: Minor HTML nesting warning: Badge (div) inside p tag causing hydration warning. This is cosmetic and doesn't affect functionality.

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/health - Returns 200 with status 'healthy'
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/dashboard - Returns comprehensive dashboard data (system_health, error_stats, auto_heal_enabled, is_running)
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/health - Returns service health status (Backend API, Frontend, MongoDB)
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/critical-modules - Returns list of protected modules (12 modules)
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/errors - Returns recent detected errors
Backend: POST /api/self-healing/toggle-auto-heal?enabled=true - Enables auto-heal successfully
Backend: POST /api/self-healing/toggle-auto-heal?enabled=false - Disables auto-heal successfully
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/rollback-stats - Returns rollback statistics
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/learning-stats - Returns learning engine statistics
Backend: POST /api/self-healing/handle-error - Submits error to self-healing pipeline successfully
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/patterns - Returns error patterns list
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/sandbox-results - Returns sandbox test results
Backend: GET /api/self-healing/reliable-patterns - Returns reliable patterns for auto-fix
Backend: All endpoints require authentication (return 401/403 without token)
Frontend: AutoHeal AI Dashboard page loads correctly
Frontend: data-testid='autoheal-dashboard' attribute present
Frontend: 6 tabs present (Real Agent, Enterprise, Overview, Reports, Alerts, Manual Diagnosis)
Frontend: Enterprise tab shows 'Enterprise Self-Healing System' header
Frontend: System Status card shows 'HEALTHY' badge
Frontend: Errors Detected stat shows count (2)
Frontend: Auto-Fix Rate stat shows percentage (0.0%)
Frontend: Protected Modules stat shows count (12)
Frontend: Service Health section shows Backend API (healthy, 20.76ms response)
Frontend: Service Health section shows Frontend (healthy, 19.86ms response)
Frontend: Service Health section shows MongoDB (healthy, 0.00ms response)
Frontend: System Metrics shows CPU Usage (3.3%)
Frontend: System Metrics shows Memory Usage (62.0%)
Frontend: System Metrics shows Disk Usage (57.0%)
Frontend: Learning Engine section present with AI-powered pattern recognition
Frontend: Rollback Manager section present with automatic reversion system
Frontend: Critical Module Protection section present
Frontend: Refresh button works
Frontend: 'Disable Auto-Heal' button present (indicating auto-heal is currently enabled)
critical_code_review_comments: Good: All self-healing core modules properly integrated (ErrorDetectionEngine, SandboxTester, CanaryDeployer, RollbackManager, CriticalModuleGuard, LearningEngine, HealthMonitor)
Good: Health monitoring checks Backend API, Frontend, and MongoDB with response times
Good: System metrics collection using psutil (CPU, Memory, Disk)
Good: Error fingerprinting and deduplication implemented
Good: Learning engine tracks pattern confidence and success rates
Good: Proper authentication required on all self-healing endpoints
Minor: Badge component inside p tag causes HTML nesting warning - consider changing p to div in AutoHealDashboard.js line 407
seed_data_creation: Error detection engine automatically detects and stores errors during normal operation
context_for_next_testing_agent: Enterprise Self-Healing System fully tested. Located at AutoHeal AI > Enterprise tab. Backend endpoints all prefixed with /api/self-healing/. Key endpoints: dashboard (GET), health (GET), critical-modules (GET), errors (GET), toggle-auto-heal (POST), rollback-stats (GET), learning-stats (GET), handle-error (POST). Frontend shows System Status, Service Health (3 services), System Metrics (CPU/Memory/Disk), Learning Engine stats, Rollback Manager stats, and Critical Module Protection list. All services currently healthy.

components.critical_module_protection: List of protected modules with protection levels (strict/standard)
components.recent_operations: List of recent healing operations with status
